key_insight: A recurrent neural network can act as a fast surrogate for laminated ferromagnetic core hysteresis and eddy-current behavior inside finite element simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables much more realistic electrical machine and magnetic device modeling at a cost close to an anhysteretic simulation, making design workflows more practical.
method: Train an RNN on diverse synthetic magnetic-field sequences that mimic machine operating conditions.
Embed the trained surrogate into 2D magnetodynamic finite element simulations using a magnetic vector potential formulation.
Use the surrogate to reproduce a reference laminated-core material model without resolving every lamination in detail.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with the reference laminated-core model while keeping runtime to about twice that of an anhysteretic simulation.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training sequences cover the operating conditions encountered in real finite element studies.
paper_title: Accounting for Hysteresis and Eddy Currents in Finite Element Simulations of Ferromagnetic Laminated Cores using a Recurrent Neural Network
